item.title: Orders-of-magnitude improvement in precision spectroscopy of an inner-shell orbital clock transition in neutral ytterbium
item.author: Taiki IshiyamaKoki OnoYoshiro Takahashi
item.content:encoded:
Precision spectroscopy of an inner-shell orbital clock transition in Yb is developed. It reveals the transition moment, the intrinsic lifetime of the excited state and the interorbital Feshbach resonance. Isotope shifts with uncertainties below 10 Hz are also measured.

Taiki IshiyamaKoki OnoYoshiro Takahashi

Nature Photonics

item.title: Exceptional sensitivity near the bistable transition point of a hybrid quantum system
item.author: Hanfeng WangKurt JacobsMatthew E. Trusheim
item.content:encoded:
Operating devices close to a phase transition can improve performance due to the singular behaviour at critical points. An enhancement in sensitivity has now been achieved using the bistable transition point in a hybrid quantum system.

Hanfeng WangKurt JacobsMatthew E. Trusheim

Nature Physics

item.title: UK bets big on homegrown fusion and quantum — can it lead the world?
item.author: David Adam
item.content:encoded:
UK government announces multibillion-pound science investments — but what impact will this have on the global race in these fields?

David Adam

Nature

item.title: Major Turing computing award goes to quantum science for first time
item.author: Davide Castelvecchi
item.content:encoded:
Turing Award winners Gilles Brassard and Charles Bennett pioneered ideas that are now foundational to quantum computers and quantum communications.

Davide Castelvecchi

Nature

item.title: Observation of self-bound droplets of ultracold dipolar molecules
item.author: Siwei ZhangWeijun YuanSebastian Will
item.content:encoded:
Self-bound droplets and droplet arrays are observed in an ultracold gas of strongly dipolar sodium–caesium molecules, establishing ultracold molecules as a system for the exploration of strongly dipolar quantum matter.

Siwei ZhangWeijun YuanSebastian Will

Nature

item.title: The E3 ubiquitin ligase mechanism specifying targeted microRNA degradation
item.author: Jakob FarnungElena SlobodyanyukDavid P. Bartel
item.content:encoded:
Target-directed microRNA degradation is driven by the atypical ZSWIM8–CUL3 E3 ubiquitin ligase that uses a two-RNA-factor authentication mechanism to specifically recognize AGO–miRNA–trigger RNA complexes and polyubiquitylate AGO.

Jakob FarnungElena SlobodyanyukDavid P. Bartel

Nature

item.title: Daily briefing: Funding calls plummet as NIH turns away from agency-directed science
item.author: Flora Graham
item.content:encoded:
The US National Institutes of Health will now focus on ‘unsolicited’ research proposals driven by the interests of individual scientists. Plus, the oldest known recording of a whale’s song and how tiny electric vehicles can help to change the world.

Flora Graham

Nature

item.title: Experimental memory control in continuous-variable optical quantum reservoir computing
item.author: Iris PaparelleJohan HenaffValentina Parigi
item.content:encoded:
Researchers demonstrate a photonic quantum reservoir computing platform that uses spectral and temporal multiplexing in a continuous-variable setting. Real-time memory is implemented, and nonlinear temporal tasks are enabled.

Iris PaparelleJohan HenaffValentina Parigi

Nature Photonics

item.title: Origin of strange metallicity in a d-orbital kagome metal
item.author: Jean C. SouzaMoshe HaimHaim Beidenkopf
item.content:encoded:
The mechanism of strange metallicity remains difficult to understand. Now it is shown that in a strongly correlated d-orbital kagome metal, compact orbitals created by destructive interference can produce the unusual electronic behaviour.

Jean C. SouzaMoshe HaimHaim Beidenkopf

Nature Physics

item.title: Bose–Hubbard simulator with long-range hopping
item.author: Camille LagoinCorentin MorinFrançois Dubin
item.content:encoded:
Simulating the Bose–Hubbard model with physical systems is an important fundamental task. Now it is shown that dipolar excitons emulate a version of this model in which bosons can hop beyond their nearest neighbours.

Camille LagoinCorentin MorinFrançois Dubin

Nature Physics

item.title: A bucket-brigade quantum random access memory
item.author: Fanhao ShenYujie JiJianwei Yin
item.content:encoded:
Random access memory has multiple data registers and uses addresses to specify which register should be read or modified. Now a quantum random access memory has been demonstrated that uses quantum addresses to return data in superposition.

Fanhao ShenYujie JiJianwei Yin

Nature Physics

item.title: Degeneracy-locked optical parametric oscillator
item.author: Fengyan YangJiacheng XieHong X. Tang
item.content:encoded:
Locking of an optical parametric oscillator’s degeneracy over a broad bandwidth enables robust operation against both pump detuning and temperature fluctuations.

Fengyan YangJiacheng XieHong X. Tang

Nature Photonics

item.title: Asymptotic quantification of entanglement with a single copy
item.author: Ludovico LamiMario BertaBartosz Regula
item.content:encoded:
Quantum information theory typically considers the asymptotic limit of having access to many copies of quantum states, which can be challenging to analyse. Now an asymptotic measure of entanglement that only needs a single copy of a quantum state has been found

Ludovico LamiMario BertaBartosz Regula

Nature Physics

item.title: Monolithic integration of continuous-variable cluster-state generation, manipulation and measurement
item.author: Xinyu JiaChang YouJianwei Wang
item.content:encoded:
Quantum photonic integrated circuits are fabricated on a Si3N4 chip. Squeezed light sources, quantum gates and a homodyne detection system are monolithically integrated. Individual qumodes and multi-qumode cluster states with high phase stability and fidelity are generated.

Xinyu JiaChang YouJianwei Wang

Nature Photonics

item.title: Entanglement quantification made simpler through hypothesis testing
item.author: N/A
item.content:encoded:
Understanding how to optimally detect and extract quantum entanglement typically involves intractable formulae that require optimization over many copies of quantum states. A shift of perspective leads to a much simpler formula based on the quantum relative entropy of a single quantum state, made possible by a new result in quantum state discrimination.

Nature Physics

item.title: One- and two-dimensional cluster states for topological phase simulation and measurement-based quantum computation
item.author: Tao JiangJianbin CaiJian-Wei Pan
item.content:encoded:
Measurement-based quantum computing implements quantum algorithms by performing sequences of measurements on special classes of entangled states, such as cluster states. This approach has now been demonstrated on a superconducting quantum processor.

Tao JiangJianbin CaiJian-Wei Pan

Nature Physics

item.title: Quantum entanglement as a tool to image distant astronomical objects
item.author: N/A
item.content:encoded:
Particles entangled over long distances can, in theory, improve the sensitivity of long-baseline interferometers that are observing weak thermal light sources. A proof-of-concept experiment now demonstrates entanglement-assisted interferometry, paving the way for entanglement-enhanced non‑local sensing techniques.

Nature

item.title: Nanophotonic waveguide chip-to-world beam scanning
item.author: Matt SahaY. Henry WenDirk Englund
item.content:encoded:
A monolithically integrated photonic ski-jump enables scalable, diffraction-limited 2D beam scanning from photonic chips, achieving ultrahigh spot rates, compact footprints and applications spanning displays, sensing and quantum photonics.

Matt SahaY. Henry WenDirk Englund

Nature

item.title: Magnetic field-induced momentum-dependent symmetry breaking in a kagome superconductor
item.author: Jianwei HuangZheng RenMing Yi
item.content:encoded:
Disentangling intertwined orders in quantum materials is challenging. Now, photoemission spectroscopy experiments show that magnetic fields can be used to disentangle such orders in a kagome superconductor.

Jianwei HuangZheng RenMing Yi

Nature Physics

item.title: Simulation of a Floquet non-Abelian topological insulator with photonic quantum walks
item.author: Quan LinTianyu LiPeng Xue
item.content:encoded:
A three-band time-multiplexed photonic quantum walk simulates a Floquet non-Abelian topological insulator. The observation of the anomalous non-Abelian phase confirms the bulk-boundary correspondence using the topological configuration of phase-band singularities.

Quan LinTianyu LiPeng Xue

Nature Photonics

item.title: Photonic ‘ski jump’ steers light beam from silicon chip
item.author: Cheryl Sorace-Agaskar
item.content:encoded:
Chip-based beam scanners use tiny electrically controlled cantilevers instead of moving mirrors to control the direction of light projected into free space.

Cheryl Sorace-Agaskar

Nature

item.title: A 1-km photonic link connecting superconducting circuits in two dilution refrigerators
item.author: Yiyu ZhouYufeng WuHong X. Tang
item.content:encoded:
The microwave-to-optical transduction between two superconducting circuits over a 1-km telecom link is implemented. The transduced signal preserves classical coherence with the original microwave signal, showing the feasibility of microwave distribution via optical carriers.

Yiyu ZhouYufeng WuHong X. Tang

Nature Photonics

item.title: Cavity-enhanced spectroscopy in the deep cryogenic regime for quantum sensing and metrology
item.author: K. StankiewiczM. MakowskiP. Wcisło
item.content:encoded:
Operating cavity-based spectrometers at low temperature has several advantages, such as improved sensitivity. Now, a cavity-enhanced spectrometer is demonstrated down to 4 K.

K. StankiewiczM. MakowskiP. Wcisło

Nature Physics

item.title: Logical multi-qubit entanglement with dual-rail superconducting qubits
item.author: Wenhui HuangXuandong SunDapeng Yu
item.content:encoded:
Quantum computers often exhibit a bias in the type of error that is the most common or severe. Entanglement has now been demonstrated for qubits encoded with an error correction code that is designed to efficiently handle biased errors.

Wenhui HuangXuandong SunDapeng Yu

Nature Physics

item.title: Kondo with a topological twist
item.author: Benjamin E. Feldman
item.content:encoded:
In a semiconductor bilayer system, local moments in one layer interact with itinerant carriers in the other to realize a two-dimensional topological Kondo insulator.

Benjamin E. Feldman

Nature Physics

item.title: High-field triplet superconductivity in a transition metal dichalcogenide superlattice
item.author: S. Y. Frank ZhaoPaul M. NevesJoseph G. Checkelsky
item.content:encoded:
BaTa2S5, which consists of alternating layers of TaS2 and Ba2TaS4, is shown to host a triplet superconducting phase at high magnetic field. This triplet phase emerges from another—more conventional—superconducting state.

S. Y. Frank ZhaoPaul M. NevesJoseph G. Checkelsky

Nature Physics

item.title: Full dimensional control of structured microwaves based on microcombs
item.author: Xiyao SongXiangpeng ZhangLin Chang
item.content:encoded:
Using a chip-based microcomb, full dimensional control of structured microwaves is demonstrated, including vortex-microwave generation, submicrosecond spatiotemporal mode switching, broadband phase–frequency response tuning and wide-angle two-dimensional beam steering. These capabilities are applied in a structured-microwave-based integrated sensing and communication system.

Xiyao SongXiangpeng ZhangLin Chang

Nature Photonics

item.title: Fresh claim of making elusive ‘hexagonal’ diamond is the strongest yet
item.author: Mark Peplow
item.content:encoded:
After decades of debate, researchers say that they have found the clearest evidence yet for this rare form of carbon.

Mark Peplow

Nature

item.title: A universal scheme to self-test any quantum state or measurement
item.author: Shubhayan SarkarAlexandre C. Orthey JrRemigiusz Augusiak
item.content:encoded:
Self-testing makes it possible to certify quantum properties without having to trust measurement or communication devices. A protocol has now been developed that allows the self-testing of any quantum state or measurement.

Shubhayan SarkarAlexandre C. Orthey JrRemigiusz Augusiak

Nature Physics

item.title: Why ‘quantum proteins’ could be the next big thing in biology
item.author: Nicola Jones
item.content:encoded:
Fluorescent proteins with a quantum upgrade could offer unprecedented views inside cells.

Nicola Jones

Nature

item.title: Daily briefing: Galileo’s notes discovered in the margins of an ancient book
item.author: Flora Graham
item.content:encoded:
Historians have discovered some recognizable jottings in a 400-year-old copy of Ptolemy’s The Almagest. Plus, tectonic plates might’ve started moving earlier than we thought and how fluorescent-protein labels can be used as quantum sensors.

Flora Graham

Nature

item.title: Entanglement-assisted non-local optical interferometry in a quantum network
item.author: P.-J. StasY.-C. WeiM. D. Lukin
item.content:encoded:
Entangled quantum memories are used in a quantum network of silicon–vacancy centres in diamond nanocavities to experimentally perform non-local phase measurements.

P.-J. StasY.-C. WeiM. D. Lukin

Nature

item.title: Quantum-limited metrology of macroscopic spin ensembles
item.author: Stephen E. KuenstnerDeclan W. SmithAlexander O. Sushkov
item.content:encoded:
Quantum fluctuations have been detected in a macroscopic, millimole-scale solid-state spin ensemble without the use of external excitations, enabling non-invasive quantum sensing techniques.

Stephen E. KuenstnerDeclan W. SmithAlexander O. Sushkov

Nature Physics

item.title: Uncovering origins of heterogeneous superconductivity in La3Ni2O7
item.author: S. V. MandyamE. WangN. Y. Yao
item.content:encoded:
Wide-field quantum sensing shows μm-scale inhomogeneous superconductivity in high-pressure La3Ni2O7, linking local diamagnetic response to stress and stoichiometry and clarifying mechanisms that suppress or enhance superconductivity.

S. V. MandyamE. WangN. Y. Yao

Nature

item.title: Field-free full switching of chiral antiferromagnetic order
item.author: Zhiyuan ZhouYanzhang CaoCheng Song
item.content:encoded:
An Mn3Sn homo-junction enables efficient field-free full switching of chiral antiferromagnets with greatly reduced power consumption, advancing their potential for energy-efficient magnetic memory devices with advantages of ultradense integration and ultrafast speed.

Zhiyuan ZhouYanzhang CaoCheng Song

Nature

item.title: Sensing with discrete time crystals
item.author: Leo Joon Il MoonPaul M. SchindlerAshok Ajoy
item.content:encoded:
It is shown that an a.c. field exponentially extends the lifetime of a prethermal time crystal realized with nuclear spins in diamond, enabling a narrowband detection of magnetic fields.

Leo Joon Il MoonPaul M. SchindlerAshok Ajoy

Nature Physics

item.title: Historically Black US universities chase top research ranking
item.author: Alexandra Witze
item.content:encoded:
Howard University is reaping the rewards of becoming the first such institution to reach ‘R1’ status.

Alexandra Witze

Nature

item.title: Five ways increased militarization could change scientific careers
item.author: Christine Ro
item.content:encoded:
Scholars working on defence, conflict and science funding consider the impact on research careers and budgets, highlighting five broad trends.

Christine Ro

Nature

item.title: Programmable integrated quantum photonics
item.author: Igor AharonovichKenneth B. CrozierDragomir Neshev
item.content:encoded:
This Review covers state-of-the-art reconfigurable and tunable optical components and highlights the emergence of a set of materials that offer a new toolkit for tunability and control.

Igor AharonovichKenneth B. CrozierDragomir Neshev

Nature Photonics

item.title: Statistical localization of U(1) lattice gauge theory in a Rydberg simulator
item.author: Prithvi Raj DatlaLuheng ZhaoHuanqian Loh
item.content:encoded:
Lattice gauge theories with non-local conservation laws are expected to thermalize locally. Using a Rydberg simulator, it is now shown that most charge patterns can remain effectively frozen, a phenomenon known as statistical localization.

Prithvi Raj DatlaLuheng ZhaoHuanqian Loh

Nature Physics

item.title: Higher-dimensional Fermiology in bulk moiré metals
item.author: Kevin P. NuckollsNisarga PaulJoseph G. Checkelsky
item.content:encoded:
Aperiodic composite crystals were discovered that emulate 2D moiré materials, demonstrating a potentially scalable approach for producing moiré materials for next-generation electronics and a generalizable approach for realizing theoretical predictions of higher-dimensional quantum phenomena.

Kevin P. NuckollsNisarga PaulJoseph G. Checkelsky

Nature

item.title: Chip-scale device efficiently boosts light signals
item.author: N/A
item.content:encoded:
Optical-amplifier devices that boost light signals are workhorses of modern technology, but shrinking them onto microchips requires impractically high power. An amplifier architecture has been developed that recycles its own ‘pump’ energy to achieve high performance at power levels ten times lower than those of previous designs, making low-power, chip-scale optical amplification practical.

Nature

item.title: Super-resolution imaging of limited-size objects
item.author: Taeyong ChangGiorgio AdamoNikolay I. Zheludev
item.content:encoded:
Limited-size object microscopy (LSOM) enables label-free super-resolution imaging of isolated nano-objects with a resolution as low as λ/8 under the sole assumption of the limited size of the imaged object.

Taeyong ChangGiorgio AdamoNikolay I. Zheludev

Nature Photonics

item.title: Quantum metrology for human health
item.author: Alex R. JonesIan S. GilmorePeter L. Knight
item.content:encoded:
Quantum technologies could be transformative for healthcare. Alex Jones, Ian Gilmore and Peter Knight discuss the role of metrology in the adoption of these technologies.

Alex R. JonesIan S. GilmorePeter L. Knight

Nature Physics

item.title: Efficient integrated quantum memory for light
item.author: Ruo-Ran MengPei-Xi LiuGuang-Can Guo
item.content:encoded:
Integrated quantum memories based on 151Eu3+:Y2SiO5 crystals coupled with impedance-matched optical cavities are demonstrated. Multiplexed quantum storage efficiencies of 80.3% and 69.8% are achieved for weak coherent pulses and telecom-heralded single photons, respectively.

Ruo-Ran MengPei-Xi LiuGuang-Can Guo

Nature Photonics

item.title: Large-scale quantum communication networks with integrated photonics
item.author: Yun ZhengHanyu WangJianwei Wang
item.content:encoded:
A lab-scale proof-of-principle demonstration of a quantum network comprising one server chip and 20 client photonic chips implementing twin-field quantum key distribution shows excellent scalability and reliability and yields a pathway towards future large-scale networks.

Yun ZhengHanyu WangJianwei Wang

Nature

item.title: Parity-doublet coherence times in optically trapped polyatomic molecules
item.author: Paige RobichaudChristian HallasJohn M. Doyle
item.content:encoded:
Optically trapped CaOH molecules in parity-doublet states achieve coherence times exceeding vibrational lifetimes, which are a defining milestone for the use of polyatomic molecules in quantum science.

Paige RobichaudChristian HallasJohn M. Doyle

Nature

item.title: Continuous-wave narrow-linewidth vacuum ultraviolet laser source
item.author: Qi XiaoGleb PenyazkovShiqian Ding
item.content:encoded:
A continuous-wave narrow-linewidth vacuum ultraviolet laser generated using four-wave mixing in cadmium vapour is shown to improve linewidth by five orders of magnitude compared with previous single-frequency lasers below 190 nm.

Qi XiaoGleb PenyazkovShiqian Ding

Nature

item.title: Single-shot parity readout of a minimal Kitaev chain
item.author: Nick van LooFrancesco ZatelliLeo P. Kouwenhoven
item.content:encoded:
A measurement strategy is described that is able to read out the parity of minimal two-site Kitaev chains in real time, by coupling two Majoranas and resolving their quantum capacitance.

Nick van LooFrancesco ZatelliLeo P. Kouwenhoven

Nature

item.title: Charge-neutral electrons are odd — except when they’re even
item.author: Andrew P. Higginbotham
item.content:encoded:
Electrons can seem electrically neutral in some devices. Neutral states can be distinguished in real time by a measure of their electron pairing called odd or even parity.

Andrew P. Higginbotham

Nature
